explanation: The property states that globally, if the system is in Standby Mode (VentilationOff) and the PCV mode change command is received from the GUI, then eventually the system must enter PCV Mode. This captures the required transition based on the given condition.

The property ensures that globally, whenever the change mode command to PCV Mode is received from the GUI and the system is currently in StandbyMode (VentilationOff), then in the very next state, the system will transition into PCV Mode. This accurately captures the conditional transition described in the natural language.

The property states that globally, if the change mode command to PCV Mode is received from the GUI while the system is in Standby Mode (VentilationOff), then eventually the system will transition into PCV Mode.

The property states that globally, if the change mode command to PCV mode ('PCVModeSelected') is received from the GUI while the system is currently in Standby Mode ('StandbyMode'), then it is guaranteed that the system will eventually enter PCV Mode ('PCVMode'). This ensures that the specified transition occurs upon the command being issued when the system is in the initial 'VentilationOff' state.

The LTL property states that globally, if the 'PCV Mode Selected' command is received from the GUI (bool_exp1) and the system is currently in 'Standby Mode' (which is equivalent to VentilationOff Mode, bool_exp2), then in the very next state (X), the system must transition to 'PCV Mode' (bool_exp3). This precisely captures the requirement for a transition from VentilationOff to PCV mode upon receiving the change mode command.

The property states that globally, if the system is in Standby Mode (VentilationOff) and the command to change to PCV Mode is received from the GUI, then in the very next state, the system will be in PCV Mode. This directly captures the required conditional transition.

The property states that globally, if the system is currently in Standby Mode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff) and the command to change to PCV Mode is received from the GUI, then it must eventually transition to and be in PCV Mode. This captures the requirement for the transition to occur from the specified initial state under the given condition.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in StandbyMode (bool_exp1) and the PCVModeSelected command is received from the GUI (bool_exp2), then in the very next state (X), the system must be in PCVMode (bool_exp3). This precisely captures the requirement for a transition from a specific mode (VentilationOff/StandbyMode) to another (PCVMode) upon a specific command.

The property states that globally, if the system is in Standby Mode (which is equivalent to VentilationOff) AND a command to change to PCV mode is received from the GUI (PCVModeSelected), then eventually the system must enter PCV Mode. This captures the requirement that the transition to PCV mode occurs upon the specific command while in the initial state.

This LTL property states that globally (G), if the change mode command to PCV Mode is received from the GUI (PCVModeSelected) AND the system is currently in Standby Mode (StandbyMode, which is equivalent to VentilationOff), then in the next state (X), the system must be in PCV Mode (PCVMode). This accurately captures the specified conditional transition from VentilationOff to PCV mode upon receiving the command.
